overall_justification: This edition strongly advocates for Nilo Peçanha's proposal for a Caixa de Conversão, emphasizing its potential benefits for the Brazilian economy, especially in conjunction with coffee valorization.  The article details how the Caixa, combined with a gold-backed currency emission, would attract foreign capital, stabilize exchange rates, stimulate credit and industry, and ultimately contribute to overall prosperity.  It frames the proposal as a pragmatic solution to Brazil's economic challenges, directly addressing concerns about currency instability and deflation.  The enthusiastic and optimistic tone of the article clearly positions the newspaper in favor of an expansionist approach.

supporting_evidence.quote: O Dr. Nilo Peçanha propõe que essas £ 15.000.000 não fossem tomadas, mas trazidas em especie, fossem cambiadas num banco de uma emissão de moeda-papel feita a uma taxa prefixada.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote introduces Peçanha's core proposal, which forms the basis of the newspaper's expansionist stance.

supporting_evidence.quote: Haverá trabalho, haverá credito, haverá prosperidade: o café beneficiará directamente dessa medida, pois que ella não será aplicada apenas a esse producto commercial, mas a todos os outros, desde 1888.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote emphatically highlights the anticipated positive economic consequences of the Caixa de Conversão, aligning with an expansionist perspective.

supporting_evidence.quote: Quando se souber que esse apparelho subtrahe o capital ouro que queira vir para o paiz às surpresas e à instabilidade da taxa cambial, esses capitaes affluirão, pois não se empregar como agora apenas em contractos com o Estado que os garante em especie para todas as funcções da vida bancaria, commercial e industrial.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This explains how the Caixa would attract foreign capital by mitigating exchange rate risks, a key argument in favor of its establishment and a central tenet of the expansionist view.
